manifest.title: U.S.S. Breckenridge: Ashes of Peace Open-World State Projection

invariants: Multiple quests may coexist; only one foreground activity is narrated at a time.
All world reactions require committed causal events.
Director-only facts never enter narrator-safe prompt blocks.
Provider output proposes state changes; deterministic code validates and commits them.
Failure creates cost or transformation rather than terminating the campaign.
SillyTavern chat is narrative evidence; structured campaign state is authoritative mechanics state.
Every model-generated quest or reconciliation change is a proposal until validated and committed through the state-delta gateway.
Recalculate From Here operates on a scratch snapshot and replaces live state only after explicit acceptance.
Dynamic quest templates are save-owned in dynamicQuestCatalog and remain resolvable after reload.
Every generated thread and quest retains source-message and source-outcome provenance.

player.role: Principal mission commander and coordinator of shipboard operations

ship.name: U.S.S. Breckenridge
ship.condition: Returned to service after a four-month repair and modernization period at Utopia Planitia; certified for service with several upgraded systems still requiring integrated validation under sustained deployment conditions.

technicalDebt.label: Integrated validation pending
technicalDebt.playerSafeSummary: The Breckenridge is certified for service, but its upgraded systems have not yet been tested together under sustained deployment conditions. High-stress operations may reveal interactions that routine checks missed.

technicalDebt.label: Command-network certificate mismatch
technicalDebt.playerSafeSummary: Command-network certificate compatibility issue exposed during recent shakedown checks between revised authentication systems and shipboard control paths. Secure command handoffs may take extra verification until Engineering remediates it.

technicalDebt.label: Combined refit load untested
technicalDebt.playerSafeSummary: Several refit discrepancies are within certified limits individually, but have not been validated together under maximum anticipated mission load. Command may need conservative operating choices when multiple upgraded systems are stressed at once.

formalObjectives: Join the U.S.S. Breckenridge as permanent Executive Officer.
Establish command rhythm with Captain Whitaker and the senior staff.
Complete routine readiness and integration work before entering the Asterion Reach.

seniorCrew.hiddenQuestion: Can this officer integrate advice and still make an independent decision?
Will the new commander encourage initiative or restrict it?
Will the commander communicate intent and trust the staff to execute it?
Can the commander defend an order when challenged by someone more experienced?
Will inconvenient evidence remain welcome once it threatens the mission?
Does the commander recognize the human cost behind operational language?
Will command respect technical limits when those limits become inconvenient?

memoryLedger.event: Reviewed the player's Starfleet service record.
memoryLedger.interpretation: The officer is qualified and has earned this posting.

memoryLedger.event: Approved or accepted the player's appointment as executive officer.
memoryLedger.interpretation: The officer deserves a genuine opportunity to command.

memoryLedger.event: The player is joining after the senior staff has spent several weeks together.
memoryLedger.interpretation: Crew integration may require deliberate handling.

unresolved: Exact starting command-culture facets after senior crew dossiers are written

active: Restore reliable Starfleet presence in the Asterion Reach.
Protect civilian life and freedom of navigation.
Survey wartime damage to communications, navigation, and subspace infrastructure.
Investigate counterfeit Starfleet signals and missing relief cargo.
Avoid prejudicing ongoing Federation negotiations concerning the Asterion Mutual Aid Compact.
Coordinate with, but not assume direct command over, local civil authorities unless a lawful emergency requires it.

canon.voyagerStatus: Voyager remains in the Delta Quadrant and does not intersect the Breckenridge campaign.

records.title: Regional Trust
records.playerSummary: Whether principal factions believe disputes can still be resolved through shared institutions.

records.title: Lantern Escalation
records.playerSummary: Pale Lantern integration and progress toward Nightfall.

records.title: Humanitarian Strain
records.playerSummary: Shortages, displacement, medical capacity, and civilian exhaustion.

records.title: Starfleet Scrutiny
records.playerSummary: Attention from admirals, intelligence offices, legal authorities, and Federation media.

records.title: Compact Unity
records.playerSummary: The condition of Compact political cohesion.

captainState.currentGoal: Integrate the new XO and determine whether the senior staff can function as a unified command team before reaching the Asterion Reach.
captainState.currentConcern: The other senior officers have already formed working relationships during the transit from Utopia Planitia, while the player is joining late. Whitaker is also watching how the player handles Bronn's temporary acting-XO legacy.

fronts.title: Lantern Integration
fronts.summary: Counterfeit systems are spreading across regional authentication and command infrastructure.
stageSummaries: Counterfeit traffic is becoming more coordinated.
Defense and evacuation systems show correlated anomalies.
The network is testing regional response times.

fronts.title: Humanitarian Strain
fronts.summary: Relief systems carry shortages, fatigue, and displacement left by the war.
stageSummaries: Evacuation and treatment capacity is strained.
Regional relief systems are near failure.

fronts.title: Compact Fracture
fronts.summary: Compact members disagree over emergency power, Starfleet authority, and postwar legitimacy.
stageSummaries: The Compact can still negotiate as a bloc.
Security and civilian leaders are dividing.
Member systems are taking unilateral positions.

fronts.title: Farwatch Evidence Purge
fronts.summary: Classified actors may destroy or compartmentalize evidence once exposure becomes likely.
stageSummaries: A purge or seizure order is ready.

fronts.title: Infrastructure Recovery
fronts.summary: Yards, relays, medical services, and civilian routes are being repaired unevenly.
stageSummaries: Essential systems operate with technical debt.
Shared infrastructure can absorb a major disruption.

clocks.title: Nightfall Readiness
clocks.playerSummary: Counterfeit incidents are becoming more coordinated.

clocks.title: Farwatch Response
clocks.playerSummary: Classified Starfleet interest in the investigation is increasing.

clocks.title: Compact Charter
clocks.playerSummary: Regional leaders are moving toward a decision about postwar authority.

instances.title: A Ship Underway
instances.playerSummary: Join the U.S.S. Breckenridge during its final ten days of transit, establish command rhythm, and respond to an ordinary civilian rescue without turning the event into a conspiracy.

objectiveStates.summary: Complete the command handover with Captain Whitaker.
Establish delegation and readiness procedures with the senior staff.
Resolve the Hesperus diversion and its maintenance fraud.
Complete the combined-load test and final command review.
Arrive at the Asterion Reach with recorded crew and ship consequences.

instances.title: The Empty Convoy
instances.playerSummary: Respond to Relief Convoy Twelve after authentic Starfleet authority appears inside a false order that dispersed ships and endangered survivors.

objectiveStates.summary: Rescue surviving convoy crews under uncertain quarantine conditions.
Separate medical, security, and evidence custody decisions.
Account for missing emergency hardware.
Create a credible incident record with local participation.
Preserve authentic code fragments embedded in the counterfeit order.

instances.title: False Colors
instances.playerSummary: Answer a public accusation that a vessel identifying itself as the Breckenridge attacked a Compact patrol while preserving medical care, independent evidence, and tactical security.

objectiveStates.summary: Stabilize the injured patrol crew.
Define transparent but secure verification terms.
Preserve independent Orison evidence.
Audit Breckenridge command systems without exposing exploitable details.
Establish a joint investigation charter.

instances.title: Dead Letters
instances.playerSummary: Recover a hazardous Hecate relay containing intercepted messages and fragments of a distributed Dominion contingency architecture.

objectiveStates.summary: Reach Hecate Seven through unstable subspace.
Recover or isolate the relay chamber.
Separate operational evidence from private civilian communications.
Prevent seizure or uncontrolled reactivation.
Determine what the architecture can and cannot prove.

instances.title: The Colony That Stayed
instances.playerSummary: Investigate Demeris, where compromised wartime authority and a diverted evacuation helped the colony survive after Starfleet failed to return.

objectiveStates.summary: Establish lawful access to the colony archive and relay workshop.
Reconstruct the diverted evacuation and Starfleet’s absence.
Determine Solenn’s use of the local interface.
Protect current community safety during public disclosure.
Create an accountability process that does not erase causation.

instances.title: Old Lessons
instances.playerSummary: Prevent a traffic and defense-platform cascade in the Orison Gap while Pale Lantern predicts Starfleet doctrine and targets authentication buoy Sigma-4.

objectiveStates.summary: Stabilize civilian traffic in the Orison Gap.
Control or disable platform O-17.
Protect authentication buoy Sigma-4.
Test Bronn’s historical model against new evidence.
Intercept or identify the Meridian and Holt’s operators.

instances.title: The Cost of Knowing
instances.playerSummary: Confront authentic classified orders, Farwatch evidence custody, and Starfleet’s decision to leave a Pale Lantern node active.

objectiveStates.summary: Verify Rourke’s authentic authority.
Prevent uncontrolled seizure or destruction of evidence.
Determine Farwatch’s operational history and warnings.
Decide what must be disclosed to regional authorities.
Preserve a usable response to the approaching Nightfall sequence.

instances.title: A Peace of Their Own
instances.playerSummary: Resolve an occupation and charter crisis that will determine who may issue legitimate regional orders during Nightfall.

objectiveStates.summary: End the Annex occupation without triggering a wider confrontation.
Define lawful participation for Compact, Starfleet, and relief authorities.
Negotiate a charter or emergency command arrangement.
Protect evidence and civilian access.
Create a command legitimacy model usable during Nightfall.

instances.title: The Last Directive
instances.playerSummary: Coordinate a distributed response to Nightfall while every automated system supplies a different reason for each actor to strike first.

objectiveStates.summary: Define the first objective and trusted command pathways.
Assign senior officers to independent fronts.
Stabilize the command mesh and civilian movement.
Control defense platforms and weapons posture.
Isolate the network quorum while protecting evacuation and relief.
Issue a final directive that allied actors can understand and verify.

instances.title: The Terms We Keep
instances.playerSummary: Settle inquiry, reconstruction, public narrative, and command consequences after Nightfall.

objectiveStates.summary: Record the operational and humanitarian aftermath.
Determine evidence custody and inquiry scope.
Set terms for Compact and Starfleet authority.
Address Farwatch and local culpability.
Review crew and command consequences.
Create persistent state for the next campaign arc.

instances.title: The Long Repair
instances.playerSummary: Turn accumulated Breckenridge technical debt into an accountable stabilization plan with Helix Yard support.

objectiveStates.summary: Inventory post-refit and mission technical debt.
Choose immediate stabilization priorities.
Assign owners to deferred risks.
Negotiate bounded Helix Yard support.

instances.title: Borrowed Wings
instances.playerSummary: Build a civilian rescue-pilot capability without pretending civilian volunteers are Starfleet crew.

objectiveStates.summary: Identify rescue pilots and craft.
Set qualification and escort limits.
Define abort and command authority.
Run a practical recovery exercise.

instances.title: Quiet Channels
instances.playerSummary: Formalize Priya’s informal communications network while deciding which favors and commitments may bind the ship.

objectiveStates.summary: Map current informal contacts and favors.
Define what Priya may promise in the ship’s name.
Test a low-bandwidth human verification chain.
Resolve one inconvenient called-in favor.

instances.title: The Last Watch
instances.playerSummary: Resolve Commander Tonn’s refusal to surrender three Orison defense platforms while preserving safety and dignity.

objectiveStates.summary: Inspect the platform network.
Expose and contain the concealed targeting fault.
Negotiate the future of the crews and platforms.
Create a transition tied to real security capacity.

instances.title: Second Opinion
instances.playerSummary: Review a trauma treatment while separating medical evidence, meaningful consent, fitness for duty, and community labor pressure.

objectiveStates.summary: Review evidence with medical experts.
Investigate coercive employment communications.
Separate treatment access from duty certification.
Set a command policy protecting meaningful consent.

instances.title: An Unwelcome Result
instances.playerSummary: Handle evidence that Hespera Colony faces long-term biosphere instability while one contaminated reference exaggerates the short-term rate.

objectiveStates.summary: Verify the environmental model.
Identify and correct the contaminated reference.
Protect residents’ informed choice.
Define certification and mitigation terms.

instances.title: The Name on the Hull
instances.playerSummary: Help define the Breckenridge’s role in a remembrance ceremony contested by survivors who reject a simple story of Starfleet protection.

objectiveStates.summary: Review the proposed ceremony.
Hear survivor objections.
Choose the ship’s public role.
Preserve remembrance without obscuring recent abandonment.

instances.title: A Signal Toward Home
instances.playerSummary: Calibrate a modest long-range subspace relay experiment connected to broader efforts to reach distant exploratory vessels.

objectiveStates.summary: Deploy three calibration beacons.
Maintain formation through phase distortion.
Coordinate with the distant research station.
Keep compromised regional traffic outside the test network.

instances.title: Two Signatures
instances.playerSummary: Support Imani while Starfleet law treats one duplicate person’s signature as sufficient authority for work created before duplication.

objectiveStates.summary: Secure duty time and legal support for Imani.
Review the agreement’s precedent-setting clauses.
Decide whether Helix Yard may use an emergency version.
Establish ship policy recognizing independent consent.

records.title: Kieran’s Command Preparation
records.observableSeed: Kieran considers an advanced command-development program and may seek review of a technically strong but personnel-blind simulation.
records.storyQuestion: Will Kieran learn to integrate people into command reasoning?
records.naturalTrigger: Downtime after a flight or command decision.

records.title: Priya’s Favors Ledger
records.observableSeed: Priya’s informal map of favors and access creates useful channels and inconvenient obligations.
records.storyQuestion: Which informal commitments count as ship obligations?
records.naturalTrigger: A contact calls in a favor or formal channels fail.

records.title: Bronn’s Table
records.observableSeed: Bronn’s late meal and Tellarite tactical game create a route to quiet mentorship and professional friendship.
records.storyQuestion: What professional trust can exist outside a crisis?
records.naturalTrigger: A late watch after the player earns Bronn’s respect.

records.title: Rowan’s Peer Review
records.observableSeed: New evidence requires Rowan to correct earlier whistleblower work without abandoning justified institutional criticism.
records.storyQuestion: Can correcting the record strengthen rather than erase principled criticism?
records.naturalTrigger: A quiet science interval after new evidence.

records.title: Miriam’s Fatigue Report
records.observableSeed: Miriam identifies normalized overwork in a department and requests a policy decision with real scheduling costs.
records.storyQuestion: What cost will command accept to stop treating overwork as normal?
records.naturalTrigger: After sustained operations, travel, or a missed rest interval.

records.title: Imani’s Comparison Problem
records.observableSeed: Messages or records confuse Imani with the other Imani Cross, creating practical and personal consequences.
records.storyQuestion: Will command recognize Imani as an independent person without reducing her to the duplication event?
records.naturalTrigger: A concrete record or message error.

records.title: Whitaker’s Correspondence
records.observableSeed: Messages from Starfleet, memorial groups, or families put symbolic expectations on Whitaker’s command.
records.storyQuestion: How does Whitaker carry symbolic leadership without surrendering judgment?

records.title: Junior-Officer Error
records.observableSeed: A junior officer conceals a recoverable mistake because they believe the XO expects flawless results.
records.storyQuestion: What has the crew learned about admitting mistakes under this command?
